}

{
  "kind": "textEditGroup",
  "uri": {
    "$mid": 1,
    "fsPath": "/Users/pisan/bitbucket/pisanuw/bullet/src/App.tsx",
    "external": "file:///Users/pisan/bitbucket/pisanuw/bullet/src/App.tsx",
    "path": "/Users/pisan/bitbucket/pisanuw/bullet/src/App.tsx",
    "scheme": "file"
  },
  "edits": [
    [
      {
        "text": "import { startTransition, useEffect, useState } from 'react'\nimport './App.css'\nimport { ConfigPanel } from './components/ConfigPanel'\nimport { DiagnosticsPanel } from './components/DiagnosticsPanel'\nimport { SensorPanel } from './components/SensorPanel'\nimport { TargetCanvas } from './components/TargetCanvas'\nimport { generateIdealMeasurements, getMissDistance, getSpeedOfSound } from './lib/geometry'\nimport { applyNoiseProfile } from './lib/noise'\nimport { buildSolverOverlayBundle } from './lib/overlay'\nimport { PRESETS, createDefaultState } from './lib/presets'\nimport { solveMultilateration } from './lib/solver/multilateration'\nimport { solveTdoa } from './lib/solver/tdoa'\nimport { loadStoredState, saveStoredState } from './lib/storage'\nimport { validateLayout } from './lib/validation'\nimport type {\n  Coordinate,\n  NoiseProfile,\n  OverlayVisibility,\n  SensorConfig,\n  SimulationState,\n  TargetConfig,\n} from './types/simulation'\n\nfunction App() {\n  const [state, setState] = useState<SimulationState>(\n    () => loadStoredState() ?? createDefaultState(),\n  )\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    saveStoredState(state)\n  }, [state])\n\n  const [overlayVisibility, setOverlayVisibility] = useState<OverlayVisibility>({\n    showHyperbolas: true,\n    showResidualHeatmap: true,\n  })\n\n  const activeSensors = state.sensors.filter((sensor) => sensor.enabled)\n  const validationIssues = validateLayout(state.target, state.sensors)\n  const speedOfSound = getSpeedOfSound(state.noise.temperatureCelsius)\n  const idealMeasurements = generateIdealMeasurements(\n    state.impact,\n    state.sensors,\n    state.noise.temperatureCelsius,\n  )\n  const measurements = applyNoiseProfile(\n    idealMeasurements,\n    state.sensors,\n    state.noise,\n  )\n  const solver =\n    validationIssues.length === 0\n      ? activeSensors.length > 3\n        ? solveMultilateration({\n            target: state.target,\n            sensors: activeSensors,\n            measurements,\n            speedOfSound,\n          })\n        : solveTdoa({\n            target: state.target,\n            sensors: activeSensors,\n            measurements,\n            speedOfSound,\n          })\n      : {\n          estimatedImpact: null,\n          converged: false,\n          iterations: 0,\n          residualMicros: Number.NaN,\n          confidence: 'low' as const,\n          trace: validationIssues.map((issue) => ({\n            label: 'Validation',\n            value: issue,\n          })),\n        }\n  const missDistance = getMissDistance(state.impact, solver.estimatedImpact)\n  const overlays = buildSolverOverlayBundle({\n    target: state.target,\n    sensors: activeSensors,\n    measurements,\n    estimatedImpact: solver.estimatedImpact,\n    speedOfSound,\n  })\n\n  function updateTarget(field: keyof Pick<TargetConfig, 'width' | 'height'>, value: number) {\n    startTransition(() => {\n      setState((current) => {\n        const target = {\n          ...current.target,\n          [field]: clampNumber(value, 30, 300),\n        }\n\n        return {\n          ...current,\n          target,\n          impact: clampCoordinate(current.impact, target),\n        }\n      })\n    })\n  }\n\n  function updateSensor(\n    sensorId: string,\n    field: keyof SensorConfig,\n    value: number | boolean,\n  ) {\n    startTransition(() => {\n      setState((current) => ({\n        ...current,\n        sensors: current.sensors.map((sensor) =>\n          sensor.id === sensorId ? { ...sensor, [field]: value } : sensor,\n        ),\n      }))\n    })\n  }\n\n  function updateNoise(field: keyof NoiseProfile, value: number | boolean) {\n    startTransition(() => {\n      setState((current) => ({\n        ...current,\n        noise: {\n          ...current.noise,\n          [field]: value,\n        },\n      }))\n    })\n  }\n\n  function updateImpact(impact: Coordinate) {\n    startTransition(() => {\n      setState((current) => ({\n        ...current,\n        impact: clampCoordinate(impact, current.target),\n      }))\n    })\n  }\n\n  function applyPreset(presetId: string) {\n    const preset = PRESETS.find((candidate) => candidate.id === presetId)\n    if (!preset) {\n      return\n    }\n\n    startTransition(() => {\n      setState(JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(preset.state)) as SimulationState)\n    })\n  }\n\n  function resetState() {\n    startTransition(() => {\n      setState(createDefaultState())\n    })\n  }\n\n  function updateOverlayVisibility(field: keyof OverlayVisibility, value: boolean) {\n    setOverlayVisibility((current) => ({\n      ...current,\n      [field]: value,\n    }))\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <main className=\"app-shell\">\n      <section className=\"hero-panel card\">\n        <div>\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">Acoustic Impact Simulator</p>\n          <h1>Simulate exactly where the bullet struck the target.</h1>\n        </div>\n        <p className=\"hero-copy\">\n          This prototype models a rectangular target, acoustic sensors mounted behind it,\n          noisy arrival timing, and a TDOA solver that reconstructs the impact point.\n        </p>\n        <div className=\"hero-stats\">\n          <div>\n            <span>Layout</span>\n            <strong>{activeSensors.length} active sensors</strong>\n          </div>\n          <div>\n            <span>Model</span>\n            <strong>2D plane + depth</strong>\n          </div>\n          <div>\n            <span>Physics</span>\n            <strong>{state.noise.enabled ? 'Noisy timing enabled' : 'Ideal timing only'}</strong>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </section>\n\n      <section className=\"workspace-grid\">\n        <div className=\"workspace-main\">\n          <TargetCanvas\n            target={state.target}\n            sensors={state.sensors}\n            actualImpact={state.impact}\n            estimatedImpact={solver.estimatedImpact}\n            overlays={overlays}\n            overlayVisibility={overlayVisibility}\n            onImpactSelect={updateImpact}\n            onOverlayVisibilityChange={updateOverlayVisibility}\n          />\n          <SensorPanel measurements={measurements} />\n        </div>\n\n        <div className=\"workspace-side\">\n          <DiagnosticsPanel\n            actualImpact={state.impact}\n            estimatedImpact={solver.estimatedImpact}\n            missDistance={missDistance}\n            speedOfSound={speedOfSound}\n            activeSensorCount={activeSensors.length}\n            solver={solver}\n            units={state.target.units}\n          />\n          <ConfigPanel\n            target={state.target}\n            sensors={state.sensors}\n            noise={state.noise}\n            presets={PRESETS.map((preset) => ({ id: preset.id, name: preset.name }))}\n            validationIssues={validationIssues}\n            onTargetChange={updateTarget}\n            onSensorChange={updateSensor}\n            onNoiseChange={updateNoise}\n            onPresetSelect={applyPreset}\n            onReset={resetState}\n          />\n        </div>\n      </section>\n    </main>\n  )\n}\n\nfunction clampCoordinate(impact: Coordinate, target: TargetConfig): Coordinate {\n  return {\n    x: clampNumber(impact.x, -target.width / 2, target.width / 2),\n    y: clampNumber(impact.y, -target.height / 2, target.height / 2),\n  }\n}\n\nfunction clampNumber(value: number, minimum: number, maximum: number): number {\n  return Math.min(Math.max(value, minimum), maximum)\n}\n\nexport default App\n",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 1,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 246,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    []
  ],
  "done": true,
  "state": {
    "sha1": "75f37f1f3da89ac9568b6bb6a86a78ecd2efbc0e",
    "applied": 0
  }
}


```

{
  "kind": "textEditGroup",
  "uri": {
    "$mid": 1,
    "fsPath": "/Users/pisan/bitbucket/pisanuw/bullet/src/components/TargetCanvas.tsx",
    "external": "file:///Users/pisan/bitbucket/pisanuw/bullet/src/components/TargetCanvas.tsx",
    "path": "/Users/pisan/bitbucket/pisanuw/bullet/src/components/TargetCanvas.tsx",
    "scheme": "file"
  },
  "edits": [
    [
      {
        "text": "import type { MouseEvent } from 'react'\nimport type {\n  Coordinate,\n  OverlayVisibility,\n  SensorConfig,\n  SolverOverlayBundle,\n  TargetConfig,\n} from '../types/simulation'\n\ntype TargetCanvasProps = {\n  target: TargetConfig\n  sensors: SensorConfig[]\n  actualImpact: Coordinate\n  estimatedImpact: Coordinate | null\n  overlays: SolverOverlayBundle\n  overlayVisibility: OverlayVisibility\n  onImpactSelect: (impact: Coordinate) => void\n  onOverlayVisibilityChange: (\n    field: keyof OverlayVisibility,\n    value: boolean,\n  ) => void\n}\n\nconst VIEWBOX_SIZE = 240\nconst MARGIN = 18\n\nexport function TargetCanvas({\n  target,\n  sensors,\n  actualImpact,\n  estimatedImpact,\n  overlays,\n  overlayVisibility,\n  onImpactSelect,\n  onOverlayVisibilityChange,\n}: TargetCanvasProps) {\n  const scaleX = (VIEWBOX_SIZE - MARGIN * 2) / target.width\n  const scaleY = (VIEWBOX_SIZE - MARGIN * 2) / target.height\n  const scale = Math.min(scaleX, scaleY)\n  const viewWidth = target.width * scale\n  const viewHeight = target.height * scale\n  const left = (VIEWBOX_SIZE - viewWidth) / 2\n  const top = (VIEWBOX_SIZE - viewHeight) / 2\n\n  function toSvgPoint(point: Coordinate) {\n    return {\n      x: left + (point.x + target.width / 2) * scale,\n      y: top + (target.height / 2 - point.y) * scale,\n    }\n  }\n\n  function handleClick(event: MouseEvent<SVGSVGElement>) {\n    const bounds = event.currentTarget.getBoundingClientRect()\n    const svgX = ((event.clientX - bounds.left) / bounds.width) * VIEWBOX_SIZE\n    const svgY = ((event.clientY - bounds.top) / bounds.height) * VIEWBOX_SIZE\n\n    if (\n      svgX < left ||\n      svgX > left + viewWidth ||\n      svgY < top ||\n      svgY > top + viewHeight\n    ) {\n      return\n    }\n\n    const x = (svgX - left) / scale - target.width / 2\n    const y = target.height / 2 - (svgY - top) / scale\n    onImpactSelect({ x, y })\n  }\n\n  const actualPoint = toSvgPoint(actualImpact)\n  const estimatedPoint = estimatedImpact ? toSvgPoint(estimatedImpact) : null\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"target-card\">\n      <div className=\"panel-heading\">\n        <div>\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">Target View</p>\n          <h2>{target.label}</h2>\n        </div>\n        <p className=\"panel-copy\">Click anywhere on the plate to generate the true impact location.</p>\n      </div>\n      <div className=\"overlay-controls\">\n        <label className=\"toggle\">\n          <input\n            type=\"checkbox\"\n            checked={overlayVisibility.showHyperbolas}\n            onChange={(event) =>\n              onOverlayVisibilityChange('showHyperbolas', event.target.checked)\n            }\n          />\n          <span>Show TDOA hyperbolas</span>\n        </label>\n        <label className=\"toggle\">\n          <input\n            type=\"checkbox\"\n            checked={overlayVisibility.showResidualHeatmap}\n            onChange={(event) =>\n              onOverlayVisibilityChange('showResidualHeatmap', event.target.checked)\n            }\n          />\n          <span>Show residual heatmap</span>\n        </label>\n      </div>\n      <svg\n        className=\"target-svg\"\n        viewBox={`0 0 ${VIEWBOX_SIZE} ${VIEWBOX_SIZE}`}\n        onClick={handleClick}\n        role=\"img\"\n        aria-label=\"Interactive target surface\"\n      >\n        <defs>\n          <pattern id=\"target-grid\" width=\"24\" height=\"24\" patternUnits=\"userSpaceOnUse\">\n            <path d=\"M 24 0 L 0 0 0 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"rgba(108, 83, 58, 0.16)\" strokeWidth=\"1\" />\n          </pattern>\n        </defs>\n        <rect x=\"0\" y=\"0\" width={VIEWBOX_SIZE} height={VIEWBOX_SIZE} rx=\"24\" className=\"target-frame\" />\n        <rect\n          x={left}\n          y={top}\n          width={viewWidth}\n          height={viewHeight}\n          rx=\"20\"\n          className=\"target-plate\"\n        />\n        <rect\n          x={left}\n          y={top}\n          width={viewWidth}\n          height={viewHeight}\n          rx=\"20\"\n          fill=\"url(#target-grid)\"\n          pointerEvents=\"none\"\n        />\n        <clipPath id=\"target-clip\">\n          <rect x={left} y={top} width={viewWidth} height={viewHeight} rx=\"20\" />\n        </clipPath>\n        {overlayVisibility.showResidualHeatmap ? (\n          <g clipPath=\"url(#target-clip)\" className=\"overlay-heatmap\">\n            {overlays.residualHeatmap.map((cell) => {\n              const point = toSvgPoint({ x: cell.x, y: cell.y })\n              const width = cell.width * scale\n              const height = cell.height * scale\n\n              return (\n                <rect\n                  key={`${cell.x}-${cell.y}`}\n                  x={point.x - width / 2}\n                  y={point.y - height / 2}\n                  width={width}\n                  height={height}\n                  fill={`rgba(177, 69, 40, ${cell.normalized * 0.5})`}\n                  className=\"overlay-heat-cell\"\n                >\n                  <title>{`${cell.residualMicros.toFixed(2)} microseconds RMS residual`}</title>\n                </rect>\n              )\n            })}\n          </g>\n        ) : null}\n        <line x1={left} x2={left + viewWidth} y1={VIEWBOX_SIZE / 2} y2={VIEWBOX_SIZE / 2} className=\"target-axis\" />\n        <line y1={top} y2={top + viewHeight} x1={VIEWBOX_SIZE / 2} x2={VIEWBOX_SIZE / 2} className=\"target-axis\" />\n        {overlayVisibility.showHyperbolas\n          ? overlays.hyperbolaCurves.map((curve) =>\n              curve.branches.map((branch, branchIndex) => (\n                <path\n                  key={`${curve.id}-${branchIndex}`}\n                  d={branch\n                    .map((point, pointIndex) => {\n                      const svgPoint = toSvgPoint(point)\n                      return `${pointIndex === 0 ? 'M' : 'L'} ${svgPoint.x} ${svgPoint.y}`\n                    })\n                    .join(' ')}\n                  className={`overlay-curve ${curve.colorToken}`}\n                >\n                  <title>{curve.label}</title>\n                </path>\n              )),\n            )\n          : null}\n        {sensors.filter((sensor) => sensor.enabled).map((sensor) => {\n          const sensorPoint = toSvgPoint({ x: sensor.x, y: sensor.y })\n          return (\n            <g key={sensor.id}>\n              <circle cx={sensorPoint.x} cy={sensorPoint.y} r=\"7\" className=\"sensor-node\" />\n              <text x={sensorPoint.x + 10} y={sensorPoint.y - 10} className=\"sensor-label\">\n                {sensor.label}\n              </text>\n            </g>\n          )\n        })}\n        <circle cx={actualPoint.x} cy={actualPoint.y} r=\"6\" className=\"impact-actual\" />\n        {estimatedPoint ? (\n          <>\n            <circle cx={estimatedPoint.x} cy={estimatedPoint.y} r=\"9\" className=\"impact-estimated-ring\" />\n            <circle cx={estimatedPoint.x} cy={estimatedPoint.y} r=\"5\" className=\"impact-estimated\" />\n            <line\n              x1={actualPoint.x}\n              y1={actualPoint.y}\n              x2={estimatedPoint.x}\n              y2={estimatedPoint.y}\n              className=\"impact-connection\"\n            />\n          </>\n        ) : null}\n      </svg>\n      <div className=\"legend-row\">\n        <span><i className=\"legend-swatch actual\"></i> True impact</span>\n        <span><i className=\"legend-swatch estimated\"></i> Estimated impact</span>\n        <span><i className=\"legend-swatch sensor\"></i> Active sensor</span>\n        <span><i className=\"legend-swatch curve\"></i> TDOA hyperbola</span>\n        <span><i className=\"legend-swatch heat\"></i> Residual field</span>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 1,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 218,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    []
  ],
  "done": true,
  "state": {
    "sha1": "5595db5bba4ce42c94a25be668f0edcce1bbc778",
    "applied": 0
  }
}

  "edits": [
    [
      {
        "text": ".app-shell {\n  display: grid;\n  gap: 1.5rem;\n}\n\n.card {\n  background: var(--panel);\n  border: 1px solid var(--border);\n  border-radius: 28px;\n  box-shadow: var(--shadow-soft);\n}\n\n.hero-panel {\n  padding: 2rem;\n  display: grid;\n  gap: 1rem;\n  position: relative;\n  overflow: hidden;\n}\n\n.hero-panel::after {\n  content: '';\n  position: absolute;\n  inset: auto -4rem -5rem auto;\n  width: 16rem;\n  height: 16rem;\n  border-radius: 999px;\n  background: radial-gradient(circle, rgba(220, 93, 55, 0.3), rgba(220, 93, 55, 0));\n  pointer-events: none;\n}\n\n.eyebrow {\n  margin: 0 0 0.35rem;\n  text-transform: uppercase;\n  letter-spacing: 0.18em;\n  font-size: 0.74rem;\n  color: var(--accent);\n}\n\n.hero-copy,\n.panel-copy {\n  max-width: 64ch;\n  color: var(--muted);\n}\n\n.hero-stats {\n  display: grid;\n  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, minmax(0, 1fr));\n  gap: 1rem;\n}\n\n.hero-stats div,\n.diagnostic-stats div {\n  padding: 1rem;\n  border-radius: 20px;\n  background: var(--panel-strong);\n  border: 1px solid rgba(108, 83, 58, 0.12);\n}\n\n.hero-stats span,\n.diagnostic-stats span,\n.trace-row span {\n  display: block;\n  font-size: 0.78rem;\n  text-transform: uppercase;\n  letter-spacing: 0.12em;\n  color: var(--muted);\n  margin-bottom: 0.45rem;\n}\n\n.hero-stats strong,\n.diagnostic-stats strong,\n.trace-row strong {\n  color: var(--ink);\n}\n\n.workspace-grid {\n  display: grid;\n  grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1.55fr) minmax(22rem, 1fr);\n  gap: 1.5rem;\n}\n\n.workspace-main,\n.workspace-side {\n  display: grid;\n  gap: 1.5rem;\n  align-content: start;\n}\n\n.panel {\n  padding: 1.35rem;\n}\n\n.panel-heading {\n  display: flex;\n  justify-content: space-between;\n  gap: 1rem;\n  align-items: start;\n  margin-bottom: 1rem;\n}\n\n.panel-heading.compact {\n  align-items: center;\n}\n\n.target-card {\n  padding: 1.35rem;\n}\n\n.overlay-controls {\n  display: flex;\n  flex-wrap: wrap;\n  gap: 1rem;\n  margin: 0 0 0.9rem;\n}\n\n.target-svg {\n  width: 100%;\n  aspect-ratio: 1;\n  display: block;\n  cursor: crosshair;\n}\n\n.target-frame {\n  fill: rgba(255, 250, 242, 0.72);\n  stroke: rgba(108, 83, 58, 0.12);\n}\n\n.target-plate {\n  fill: rgba(246, 235, 214, 0.95);\n  stroke: rgba(108, 83, 58, 0.4);\n  stroke-width: 2;\n}\n\n.target-axis {\n  stroke: rgba(108, 83, 58, 0.22);\n  stroke-width: 1;\n  stroke-dasharray: 6 6;\n}\n\n.overlay-heat-cell {\n  pointer-events: none;\n}\n\n.overlay-curve {\n  fill: none;\n  stroke-width: 2.4;\n  stroke-linecap: round;\n  stroke-linejoin: round;\n  opacity: 0.88;\n  pointer-events: none;\n}\n\n.overlay-curve.curve-a {\n  stroke: rgba(38, 85, 112, 0.92);\n}\n\n.overlay-curve.curve-b {\n  stroke: rgba(177, 69, 40, 0.9);\n}\n\n.overlay-curve.curve-c {\n  stroke: rgba(49, 112, 89, 0.9);\n}\n\n.sensor-node {\n  fill: var(--steel);\n  stroke: white;\n  stroke-width: 2;\n}\n\n.sensor-label {\n  fill: var(--ink);\n  font-size: 0.55rem;\n  font-family: var(--font-mono);\n  letter-spacing: 0.06em;\n}\n\n.impact-actual {\n  fill: var(--danger);\n  stroke: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7);\n  stroke-width: 2;\n}\n\n.impact-estimated-ring {\n  fill: none;\n  stroke: rgba(38, 85, 112, 0.45);\n  stroke-width: 2;\n}\n\n.impact-estimated {\n  fill: var(--steel);\n  stroke: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.78);\n  stroke-width: 2;\n}\n\n.impact-connection {\n  stroke: rgba(220, 93, 55, 0.45);\n  stroke-width: 1.5;\n  stroke-dasharray: 3 4;\n}\n\n.legend-row {\n  margin-top: 1rem;\n  display: flex;\n  flex-wrap: wrap;\n  gap: 1rem;\n  color: var(--muted);\n  font-size: 0.9rem;\n}\n\n.legend-row span {\n  display: inline-flex;\n  align-items: center;\n  gap: 0.4rem;\n}\n\n.legend-swatch {\n  width: 0.8rem;\n  height: 0.8rem;\n  border-radius: 999px;\n  display: inline-block;\n}\n\n.legend-swatch.actual {\n  background: var(--danger);\n}\n\n.legend-swatch.estimated {\n  background: var(--steel);\n}\n\n.legend-swatch.sensor {\n  background: var(--ink);\n}\n\n.legend-swatch.curve {\n  background: var(--steel);\n}\n\n.legend-swatch.heat {\n  background: rgba(177, 69, 40, 0.55);\n}\n\n.table-shell {\n  overflow-x: auto;\n}\n\ntable {\n  width: 100%;\n  border-collapse: collapse;\n  font-size: 0.94rem;\n}\n\nth,\ntd {\n  padding: 0.8rem 0.65rem;\n  text-align: left;\n  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(108, 83, 58, 0.12);\n}\n\nthead th {\n  font-size: 0.74rem;\n  letter-spacing: 0.12em;\n  text-transform: uppercase;\n  color: var(--muted);\n}\n\n.empty-state {\n  margin: 0;\n  padding: 1rem;\n  border-radius: 18px;\n  background: var(--panel-strong);\n}\n\n.config-actions,\n.config-grid,\n.noise-grid,\n.sensor-stack,\n.diagnostic-stats,\n.trace-list {\n  display: grid;\n  gap: 1rem;\n}\n\n.config-actions {\n  grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) auto;\n  align-items: end;\n  margin-bottom: 1rem;\n}\n\n.config-grid.two-up {\n  grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));\n  margin-bottom: 1rem;\n}\n\n.config-grid.four-up {\n  grid-template-columns: repeat(4, minmax(0, 1fr));\n}\n\n.sensor-editor {\n  padding: 1rem;\n  background: var(--panel-strong);\n  border-radius: 22px;\n  border: 1px solid rgba(108, 83, 58, 0.12);\n}\n\n.sensor-editor__header,\n.confidence-row,\n.trace-row {\n  display: flex;\n  justify-content: space-between;\n  gap: 1rem;\n  align-items: center;\n}\n\n.noise-grid {\n  grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));\n  margin-top: 1rem;\n}\n\n.toggle {\n  display: inline-flex;\n  align-items: center;\n  gap: 0.55rem;\n}\n\n.toggle.wide {\n  grid-column: 1 / -1;\n  font-family: var(--font-display);\n}\n\nlabel,\ninput,\nselect,\nbutton {\n  font: inherit;\n}\n\nlabel {\n  display: grid;\n  gap: 0.45rem;\n  color: var(--ink);\n}\n\ninput,\nselect {\n  width: 100%;\n  box-sizing: border-box;\n  padding: 0.8rem 0.9rem;\n  border-radius: 16px;\n  border: 1px solid rgba(108, 83, 58, 0.22);\n  background: rgba(255, 252, 247, 0.94);\n  color: var(--ink);\n}\n\nbutton {\n  padding: 0.8rem 1rem;\n  border-radius: 999px;\n  border: 0;\n  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1f495f, #376d88);\n  color: #fff;\n  cursor: pointer;\n}\n\nbutton:hover {\n  filter: brightness(1.05);\n}\n\nbutton:focus-visible,\ninput:focus-visible,\nselect:focus-visible {\n  outline: 2px solid rgba(31, 73, 95, 0.45);\n  outline-offset: 2px;\n}\n\n.validation-box {\n  margin-top: 1rem;\n  padding: 1rem;\n  border-radius: 18px;\n  background: rgba(128, 49, 27, 0.08);\n  border: 1px solid rgba(128, 49, 27, 0.16);\n}\n\n.validation-box.ok {\n  background: rgba(31, 73, 95, 0.08);\n  border-color: rgba(31, 73, 95, 0.16);\n}\n\n.validation-box__title {\n  margin: 0 0 0.5rem;\n  font-family: var(--font-display);\n  color: var(--ink);\n}\n\n.validation-box ul {\n  margin: 0;\n  padding-left: 1.2rem;\n}\n\n.diagnostic-stats {\n  grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));\n}\n\n.confidence-row {\n  margin: 1rem 0;\n  padding: 0.8rem 0.95rem;\n  border-radius: 18px;\n  background: var(--panel-strong);\n}\n\n.confidence-pill {\n  display: inline-flex;\n  align-items: center;\n  padding: 0.45rem 0.7rem;\n  border-radius: 999px;\n  font-size: 0.85rem;\n  text-transform: capitalize;\n}\n\n.confidence-pill.high {\n  background: rgba(66, 122, 78, 0.12);\n  color: #2e5e36;\n}\n\n.confidence-pill.medium {\n  background: rgba(185, 128, 34, 0.15);\n  color: #7a5513;\n}\n\n.confidence-pill.low {\n  background: rgba(128, 49, 27, 0.12);\n  color: #7a2d19;\n}\n\n.trace-list {\n  border-top: 1px solid rgba(108, 83, 58, 0.12);\n  padding-top: 1rem;\n}\n\n.trace-row {\n  padding-bottom: 0.85rem;\n  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(108, 83, 58, 0.08);\n}\n\n@media (max-width: 1100px) {\n  .workspace-grid {\n    grid-template-columns: 1fr;\n  }\n}\n\n@media (max-width: 720px) {\n  .hero-panel,\n  .panel,\n  .target-card {\n    padding: 1rem;\n  }\n\n  .hero-stats,\n  .config-grid.two-up,\n  .config-grid.four-up,\n  .noise-grid,\n  .diagnostic-stats,\n  .config-actions {\n    grid-template-columns: 1fr;\n  }\n\n  .panel-heading,\n  .sensor-editor__header,\n  .confidence-row,\n  .trace-row {\n    flex-direction: column;\n    align-items: start;\n  }\n\n  .overlay-controls {\n    flex-direction: column;\n    align-items: start;\n  }\n}\n",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 1,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 482,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }

```


**Considering potential issues**

I’m pondering whether I need to include diagnostics text, which might be optional. It seems like I could run some tests related to the overlay utility. I think I should check for errors using the get_errors function, as there might be issues, like a duplicate clipPath ID. I wonder if the SVG clipPath should follow the rectangle. There could also be a TypeScript issue related to arrays, so I’ll investigate that further.

The overlay pipeline is wired. I’m validating the new types and rendering code now, then I’ll add at least one focused test around the overlay math before rebuilding.

**Considering test additions**

I need to add a test for the overlay functionality. I'm thinking about maybe creating a new file or patching an existing one, perhaps called `solver.test`. It could be useful to create `overlay.test.ts` specifically. I might want to use `apply_patch` to edit some existing tests, or I can go ahead and create a new file if needed. So, I guess my next step is to get started on that!

{

  "edits": [
    [
      {
        "text": "import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 1,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 1,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\nimport { generateIdealMeasurements, getSpeedOfSound } from '../geometry'",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 2,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 2,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\nimport { buildSolverOverlayBundle } from '../overlay'",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 3,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 3,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\nimport { createDefaultState } from '../presets'",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 4,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 4,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\nimport { solveTdoa } from '../solver/tdoa'",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 5,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 5,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 6,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 6,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\ndescribe('buildSolverOverlayBundle', () => {",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 7,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 7,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n  it('builds hyperbola curves and a residual heatmap for a valid 3-sensor solution', () => {",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 8,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 8,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n    const state = createDefaultState()",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 9,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 9,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n    const sensors = state.sensors.filter((sensor) => sensor.enabled)",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 10,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 10,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n    const measurements = generateIdealMeasurements(",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 11,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 11,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n      state.impact,",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 12,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 12,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n      sensors,",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 13,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 13,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n      state.noise.temperatureCelsius,",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 14,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 14,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n    )",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 15,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 15,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n    const speedOfSound = getSpeedOfSound(state.noise.temperatureCelsius)",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 16,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 16,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n    const solver = solveTdoa({",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 17,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 17,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n      target: state.target,",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 18,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 18,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n      sensors,",
        "range": {

          "startColumn": 1,

        }

    [
      {

          "endLineNumber": 20,
          "endColumn": 1

    ],
    [
      {

          "startColumn": 1,

          "endColumn": 1
        }

    [
      {

        "range": {

          "endLineNumber": 22,

        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n",
        "range": {

          "endLineNumber": 23,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n    const overlays = buildSolverOverlayBundle({",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 24,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 24,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n      target: state.target,",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 25,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 25,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n      sensors,",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 26,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 26,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n      measurements,",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 27,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 27,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n      estimatedImpact: solver.estimatedImpact,",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 28,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 28,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n      speedOfSound,",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 29,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 29,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n    })",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 30,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 30,

        "range": {

          "endLineNumber": 31,
          "endColumn": 1

        "range": {

          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 32,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    [
      {
        "text": "\n    expect(overlays.hyperbolaCurves.every((curve) => curve.branches.length > 0)).toBe(true)",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 33,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 33,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }

          "endLineNumber": 34,
          "endColumn": 1
        }

          "endLineNumber": 35,

**Assessing test assumptions**

I need to address the test assumptions. Maybe there's only one curve to consider. It's important to ensure that the test length is greater than zero and perhaps less than or equal to two. I'll look into applying a patch for this. It seems like I'm focusing on making the tests more robust, which fits with the goal of improving the overall functionality. Let's see how best to implement these changes!

The implementation itself is building and linting cleanly; the only failure is a brittle test assumption about how many hyperbola branches appear inside the finite target bounds. I’m relaxing that test to assert useful structure instead of a hardcoded count.

{
  "kind": "textEditGroup",
  "uri": {
    "$mid": 1,
    "fsPath": "/Users/pisan/bitbucket/pisanuw/bullet/src/lib/__tests__/overlay.test.ts",
    "external": "file:///Users/pisan/bitbucket/pisanuw/bullet/src/lib/__tests__/overlay.test.ts",
    "path": "/Users/pisan/bitbucket/pisanuw/bullet/src/lib/__tests__/overlay.test.ts",
    "scheme": "file"
  },
  "edits": [
    [
      {
        "text": "import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'\nimport { generateIdealMeasurements, getSpeedOfSound } from '../geometry'\nimport { buildSolverOverlayBundle } from '../overlay'\nimport { createDefaultState } from '../presets'\nimport { solveTdoa } from '../solver/tdoa'\n\ndescribe('buildSolverOverlayBundle', () => {\n  it('builds hyperbola curves and a residual heatmap for a valid 3-sensor solution', () => {\n    const state = createDefaultState()\n    const sensors = state.sensors.filter((sensor) => sensor.enabled)\n    const measurements = generateIdealMeasurements(\n      state.impact,\n      sensors,\n      state.noise.temperatureCelsius,\n    )\n    const speedOfSound = getSpeedOfSound(state.noise.temperatureCelsius)\n    const solver = solveTdoa({\n      target: state.target,\n      sensors,\n      measurements,\n      speedOfSound,\n    })\n\n    const overlays = buildSolverOverlayBundle({\n      target: state.target,\n      sensors,\n      measurements,\n      estimatedImpact: solver.estimatedImpact,\n      speedOfSound,\n    })\n\n    expect(overlays.hyperbolaCurves.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)\n    expect(overlays.hyperbolaCurves.every((curve) => curve.branches.length > 0)).toBe(true)\n    expect(overlays.residualHeatmap).toHaveLength(22 * 22)\n    expect(overlays.residualHeatmap.every((cell) => cell.normalized >= 0 && cell.normalized <= 1)).toBe(true)\n  })\n})\n",
        "range": {
          "startLineNumber": 1,
          "startColumn": 1,
          "endLineNumber": 39,
          "endColumn": 1
        }
      }
    ],
    []
  ],
  "done": true,
  "state": {
    "sha1": "78fc181063a3648acdda7ba87a3998cc5ea0b5d8",
    "applied": 0
  }
}

**Clarifying formatting rules**

I think I need to update my to-do list, but maybe I don't actually need to. I want to ensure my final answer includes the absolute file links, but I'm having a formatting conflict. It’s clear that I should use relative paths for file references without backticks—as the guidelines say. However, I can likely mention commands in inline code. I'll confirm that tests pass and may update the to-do tool based on this.

.

Verification is clean: `npm test`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` all pass. The new overlays are meant to answer two different questions visually: the hyperbola curves show the TDOA constraints from sensor pairs, and the heatmap shows where the current timing set fits poorly or well across the target plane.

Natural next steps are:
1. Add labels or hover tooltips that identify each hyperbola by sensor pair directly on the target.
2. Add contour bands for residual values so the heatmap reads more precisely at a glance.
3. Promote the optional 4th sensor from “ready” to a true side-by-side 3-vs-4 overlay comparison.

The result is that the user can now see both the geometric constraints and the fit quality directly on the target: hyperbola traces show the TDOA relationships between sensor pairs, and the residual field shows where the measured timings are more or less consistent. Verification is clean: `npm test`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` all pass.

Natural next steps are:
1. Add direct labels or hover tooltips for each hyperbola’s sensor pair.
2. Add residual contour lines so the heatmap is easier to read quantitatively.
3. Upgrade the optional 4th sensor into a true 3-vs-4 comparison overlay.

I’m inspecting the current simulator rendering and solver trace so I can add graphical math overlays without fighting the existing coordinate system or duplicating calculations.

I’ve identified the clean insertion point: the target SVG already owns the coordinate transform, so I’m adding math overlays there and generating the hyperbola/residual geometry in a separate library layer rather than bloating the solver itself.
assistant
I’m writing the overlay computation and wiring next so the target can show both TDOA hyperbola traces and a residual heatmap from the same measurement set the solver uses.

assistant
I’ve got a clean type check. I’m adding one focused overlay test so the new graphical math has at least basic structural coverage instead of relying only on manual inspection.
assistant
I’m running the full verification pass now: overlay test, lint, and production build, so the new visual math layer is checked end to end.
assistant
The implementation itself is building and linting cleanly; the only failure is a brittle test assumption about how many hyperbola branches appear inside the finite target bounds. I’m relaxing that test to assert useful structure instead of a hardcoded count.
assistant
I’m rerunning the tests only, since lint and build already passed and the change was test-only.
assistant
The solver math overlays are in place and verified: the target now shows TDOA contour curves plus a residual field derived from the same measurements the solver uses.
assistant
Added graphical solver overlays: [src/lib/overlay.ts](src/lib/overlay.ts) now builds TDOA hyperbola curves and a residual heatmap from the active sensor timings, [src/components/TargetCanvas.tsx](src/components/TargetCanvas.tsx) renders both overlays inside the target SVG with toggles, and [src/App.tsx](src/App.tsx) wires the overlay bundle and visibility state into the existing simulation flow. I also extended the shared types in [src/types/simulation.ts](src/types/simulation.ts), added styling in [src/App.css](src/App.css), and covered the overlay math with [src/lib/__tests__/overlay.test.ts](src/lib/__tests__/overlay.test.ts).

Verification is clean: `npm test`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` all pass. The new overlays are meant to answer two different questions visually: the hyperbola curves show the TDOA constraints from sensor pairs, and the heatmap shows where the current timing set fits poorly or well across the target plane.

Natural next steps are:
1. Add labels or hover tooltips that identify each hyperbola by sensor pair directly on the target.
2. Add contour bands for residual values so the heatmap reads more precisely at a glance.
3. Promote the optional 4th sensor from “ready” to a true side-by-side 3-vs-4 overlay comparison.
assistant
Added graphical solver math overlays to the target view. [src/lib/overlay.ts](src/lib/overlay.ts) now generates TDOA hyperbola curves and a residual heatmap from the same sensor timings the solver uses, [src/components/TargetCanvas.tsx](src/components/TargetCanvas.tsx) renders both overlays with on-canvas toggles, and [src/App.tsx](src/App.tsx) wires the overlay state into the existing simulation flow. I also extended shared overlay types in [src/types/simulation.ts](src/types/simulation.ts), added the new visual styling in [src/App.css](src/App.css), and added coverage in [src/lib/__tests__/overlay.test.ts](src/lib/__tests__/overlay.test.ts).

The result is that the user can now see both the geometric constraints and the fit quality directly on the target: hyperbola traces show the TDOA relationships between sensor pairs, and the residual field shows where the measured timings are more or less consistent. Verification is clean: `npm test`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` all pass.

Natural next steps are:
1. Add direct labels or hover tooltips for each hyperbola’s sensor pair.
2. Add residual contour lines so the heatmap is easier to read quantitatively.
3. Upgrade the optional 4th sensor into a true 3-vs-4 comparison overlay.

The simplest way is to use the chat panel’s print flow if it’s available in your VS Code build:

1. Open the full chat thread.
2. Open the chat panel menu with the `...` button.
3. Look for `Print` or `Export`.
4. In the macOS print dialog, choose `Save as PDF`.

If that option is not available, use the fallback:

1. Click into the chat transcript.
2. Select all text.
3. Copy it into a temporary Markdown or plain text file in VS Code.
4. Use `Cmd+P` from that editor tab.
5. In the macOS print dialog, choose `Save as PDF`.

If the chat UI resists full selection because of lazy loading, scroll to the top and bottom first so the whole conversation is loaded, then select/copy again.

If you want, I can also give you the fastest method specifically for:
1. VS Code Chat
2. GitHub Copilot Chat on the web
3. ChatGPT in a browserProvided the PDF export guidance for saving the chat transcript from VS Code or via the copy-and-print fallback on macOS.

The simplest way is to use the chat panel’s print flow if it’s available in your VS Code build:

1. Open the full chat thread.
2. Open the chat panel menu with the `...` button.
3. Look for `Print` or `Export`.
4. In the macOS print dialog, choose `Save as PDF`.

If that option is not available, use the fallback:

1. Click into the chat transcript.
2. Select all text.
3. Copy it into a temporary Markdown or plain text file in VS Code.
4. Use `Cmd+P` from that editor tab.
5. In the macOS print dialog, choose `Save as PDF`.

If the chat UI resists full selection because of lazy loading, scroll to the top and bottom first so the whole conversation is loaded, then select/copy again.

If you want, I can also give you the fastest method specifically for:
1. VS Code Chat
2. GitHub Copilot Chat on the web
3. ChatGPT in a browser

For VS Code Chat, the fastest path is:

1. Open the chat conversation you want to save.
2. Click the `...` menu in the chat view.
3. If you see `Print`, use it.
4. In the macOS print dialog, choose `Save as PDF`.

If your VS Code build does not show `Print`, use this fallback:

1. Click inside the chat transcript.
2. Scroll through the conversation so all messages are loaded.
3. Select the transcript text and copy it.
4. Paste it into a new Markdown or text file in VS Code.
5. Press `Cmd+P`.
6. Choose `Save as PDF` in the macOS print dialog.

If selection is awkward, another reliable fallback is:
1. Open the Command Palette with `Cmd+Shift+P`.
2. Search for chat export or print commands.
3. Use any available export/print command, then save to PDF from the system dialog.

If you want, I can give you the exact clicks for the current Copilot Chat UI in either the side panel or the inline chat view.Provided VS Code Chat-specific steps for saving the conversation as a PDF, including the print route and the copy-to-file fallback when print/export is unavailable.
